title: spergula arvensis subsp. chieusseana
intro: Spergula arvensis subsp. chieusseana, commonly known as the Corn Spurry, is a flowering plant in the purslane family that grows in a variety of habitats and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and a sedative. The health benefits of Spergula arvensis subsp. chieusseana include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce anxiety and insomnia, and alleviate symptoms of colds and fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
